name: SLB Limited

profile.description: SLB is the world's largest oilfield services company, operating through four divisions: Digital & Integration, Reservoir Performance, Well Construction, and Production Systems. The company rebranded from Schlumberger in 2022 to signal its evolution toward digital and energy technology services. FY2025 revenue was $35.71B with $4.1B in free cash flow. The ChampionX acquisition adds production chemistry capabilities, and the Digital & Integration segment generates $926M ARR at 32.7% EBITDA margins. CEO Olivier Le Peuch is driving a pivot toward AI-powered services, carbon capture, and geothermal, targeting $10B in new energy revenue by 2040.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track record (2/3): SLB's 2022 rebranding from Schlumberger to an 'energy technology company,' combined with the launch of Delfi cognitive E&P environment, Lumi generative AI platform, and Tela AI assistant, represents a genuine business model transformation from field services to tech-enabled energy solutions [1][2]. Digital & Integration segment achieved $926M ARR with 32.7% EBITDA margins — the highest in the portfolio. AI deployment (2/2): SLB's AI tools have delivered a documented 23.4% efficiency improvement in customer operations; Lumi AI platform is shipping to E&P operators with quantifiable results; combined digital revenue approaches $3B annually [2]. Market share (2/2): SLB holds #1 or #2 positions in virtually every global oilfield service line, and its digital platform leads the energy technology space. Pipeline (0/2): new energy revenue (CCS, geothermal, critical minerals, data center solutions) exceeded $1B in 2025, but specific next-gen product launch dates are limited. R&D intensity (1/1): SLB invests above industry average in R&D across digital, new energy, and core services. Total: 2+2+2+0+1 = 7.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission floor (3/4): SLB's evolving purpose — 'unlocking access to energy for the benefit of all' while transitioning the energy system — reflects genuine strategic intentionality validated by the 2022 rebranding, $1B+ new energy investment, and 30% Scope 1/2 emissions reduction target [2][3]. CEO Le Peuch has consistently articulated this as the organizing principle for capital allocation. Community impact (1/2): SLB has supplier diversity programs and community development in 100+ operating countries, but lacks distinctive large-scale charitable initiatives beyond industry norms. Employee retention (1/2): Le Peuch's tenure (CEO since 2019, at SLB for decades) and the company's history of promoting from within across a 109,000-person global workforce reflect organizational stability; SLB ranks #1 in Overall Culture Score on Comparably vs competitors [2]. Insider buying (1/2): Le Peuch and senior leadership maintain significant equity positions aligned with the transformation strategy, though open-market purchases are limited. Total: 3+1+1+1 = 6.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: SLB is a Dividend stock (yield 2.06%, 45+ consecutive years paid). Calculated mechanically: D/E 42.52% scores 1.0. ROE 14.07% scores 0.938. Revenue growth YoY -1.6% (FY2025 vs FY2024) scores 0.613. Revenue 5yr CAGR 8.6% scores 0.907. Forward P/E 17.11 scores 1.0. Profit margin 9.26% scores 0.463. Payout ratio 50.66% scores 0.822 (1x). Consecutive years paid 45+ scores 1.0 (2x). Consecutive years growth 4 scores 0.400 (1x). 5yr dividend growth CAGR 22.9% scores 1.0 (2x). Weighted sum: 1.0+0.938+0.613+0.907+1.0+0.463+0.822+(1.0x2)+0.400+(1.0x2) = 10.14/12 -> score 8. Liquidity: $57.28 x 16.3M avg volume = $936M daily, passes floor [1][4].

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: SLB's core business — specialized oilfield services, subsurface imaging, drilling technology, and production chemistry — requires deep physical-world expertise and proprietary tools that AI cannot replace [1]. More importantly, SLB IS an AI provider for the energy industry: Delfi, Lumi AI platform, and Tela AI assistant generate nearly $3B in digital-related annual revenue with a documented 23.4% customer efficiency improvement [2]. CEO Le Peuch explicitly describes AI as 'the X factor for our industry,' and SLB is building AI infrastructure for energy operators, including data center solutions generating $1B+ revenue. AI Risk Protection: 7/9 (PROTECTED — physical operations are immune; digital platform leadership is a defensive moat). AI Opportunity: 1/1 (Lumi AI platform, Delfi, and digital services generate quantifiable AI-related revenue approaching $3B). Total: 8.

infinite_game.summary: SLB's 2022 rebranding — voluntarily walking away from the century-old 'Schlumberger' identity to become an energy technology company — is a textbook act of Existential Flexibility in service of a Just Cause. CEO Le Peuch's tenure and the $10B new energy revenue target by 2040 reflect genuine long-horizon strategic thinking. The company is investing in carbon capture, geothermal, and critical minerals before these markets have proven commercial returns, demonstrating courage to lead.

just_cause.analysis: SLB's Just Cause — 'unlocking access to energy for the benefit of all' while decarbonizing the energy system — reflects genuine strategic intentionality validated by the $1B+ new energy revenue milestone and the radical rebranding decision [2]. The new energy businesses (carbon capture, geothermal, critical minerals) demonstrate commitment to the energy transition even when markets are unproven. Scores 2/3: genuine cause validated by costly strategic decisions, approaching but not yet at multi-generational consistency.

trusting_teams.analysis: SLB operates one of the most diverse, multinational workforces in the industrial sector — 109,000 employees across 100+ countries with deep local expertise. CEO Le Peuch has been at SLB for decades before becoming CEO, and the company has a strong promote-from-within culture. SLB ranks #1 in Overall Culture Score on Comparably vs competitors, and the rebranding was managed without major cultural disruption [2]. Scores 2/2.

worthy_rivals.analysis: SLB explicitly benchmarks against Halliburton, Baker Hughes, and emerging technology-focused energy companies. The digital pivot was partly inspired by recognition that technology companies would disrupt legacy field services if incumbents didn't adapt — a worthy-rival mindset in practice [1][2]. Scores 1/1.

existential_flexibility.analysis: The 2022 rebranding from the century-old 'Schlumberger' identity to 'SLB,' combined with the ChampionX acquisition for production chemistry and the $1B+ new energy revenue (CCS, geothermal, critical minerals, data center solutions), represents genuine existential flexibility [1][2]. SLB is deliberately eroding its legacy oilfield services identity while building a new energy technology platform — exactly what Sinek describes. Scores 2/2.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Investing in carbon capture and geothermal before proven commercial returns, voluntarily abandoning the iconic 'Schlumberger' brand, and committing $4B+ to shareholder returns in 2026 while simultaneously investing in unproven new energy markets all demonstrate institutional courage [1][2]. However, the core fossil fuel services business remains the vast majority of revenue, suggesting courage is bounded by financial pragmatism. Scores 1/2.

investment_thesis: SLB is the world's highest-quality energy technology company trading at 17.1x forward P/E with a 2.06% dividend yield and $4.1B in annual free cash flow [1]. The Digital & Integration segment — generating $926M ARR at 32.7% EBITDA margins — trades at an implicit discount to SaaS peers despite strong growth. The ChampionX acquisition adds $400M in annual pretax synergies by 2028 and is accretive to 2026 EPS [1]. The ALIGNED Inspire score of +51 with zero negative attributions makes SLB one of the most values-compatible energy holdings in the IOWN universe. Committed $4B+ shareholder returns in 2026 with room for continued dividend growth [4].
thesis_continued: The primary risk is oilfield activity cyclicality: FY2025 revenue declined 1.6%, and Q1 2026 revenue excluding ChampionX declined 7% YoY as international spending moderated [1]. SLB's heavy international exposure (75%+ of revenue) introduces geopolitical risk across Latin America, Middle East, and Africa. The digital business — while growing 17% YoY — remains a small fraction of total revenue; the transformation to an energy technology company is real but will take 5-10 years to fully shift the margin profile. The trailing P/E of 25.2x and PEG of 1.97 suggest the market already prices significant transformation premium, limiting near-term upside if execution falters. Sustained oil prices below $55/bbl would materially reduce upstream spending and compress SLB earnings by 15-25%.
key_catalysts: ChampionX integration delivering $400M in annual synergies by 2028, with 2026 EPS accretion providing near-term positive earnings revisions [1]
Digital & Integration ARR growing from $926M toward $1.5B — each incremental ARR dollar at 32.7% EBITDA margins is worth 5-7x revenue multiple versus traditional field services at 1-2x [1]
New energy revenue exceeding $1.5B in 2026 (up from $1B+ in 2025) — carbon capture, geothermal, and data center solutions validating the energy technology pivot
key_risks: Oilfield services cyclicality: Q1 2026 core revenue declined 7% ex-ChampionX; sustained oil prices below $55/bbl would trigger 15-25% earnings compression as national oil companies and IOCs cut spending [1]

International geopolitical risk: 75%+ of revenue from markets including Latin America, Middle East, Africa, and Russia — political instability, sanctions, or currency depreciation could materially impact results
Transformation timeline: digital revenue remains a small fraction of $35.7B total — the technology pivot may take another 5-10 years to fully shift SLB's margin and growth profile [1]
sources: SLB Q4 and Full Year 2025 Earnings Release and Q1 2026 Earnings Release, SEC filings, January-April 2026
SLB AI strategy, Lumi platform, and corporate culture — AI Magazine, OGR Energy, Comparably, SLB.com, 2025-2026
SLB ESG and sustainability profile — Sustainalytics (Low Controversy), MSCI AA rating, offshore-technology.com
Yahoo Finance — SLB key statistics and valuation, accessed May 2026
